practice: Describe the steps in the Quick Start automated scan.
Explain when to choose the traditional spider vs the AJAX spider.
List two reasons to run a scan only with explicit permission.
title: ZAP Lesson 3: Quick Start Automated Scan
summary: Run a Quick Start automated scan and understand spider options.
content:
# ZAP Lesson 3: Quick Start Automated Scan

## Overview
Quick Start provides an automated scan workflow. You enter a target URL, choose scan options, and ZAP handles crawling and testing.

## Automated scan steps
1. Open the Quick Start tab
2. Choose Automated Scan
3. Enter the target URL you are authorized to test
4. Select spider options if needed
5. Start the scan and review alerts

## Spider choices
- Traditional spider: fast, works best on classic HTML navigation
- AJAX spider: slower, uses a browser to explore JavaScript-heavy apps

## After the scan
Review alerts, confirm false positives, and prioritize by risk and confidence.

## CLI alternative
For CI pipelines, packaged scripts can run scans from the command line using ZAP Docker images.
examples: Run a traditional spider for a server-rendered app.
Use the AJAX spider for a single-page application.
Export an HTML report after scanning a test environment.
objectives: Perform a Quick Start automated scan.
